GRHMORXB Gresham Central Office, 150 NW 20th St, telco central office / colo (PeeringDB fac/14267, now WholeSail/Ziply, formerly Zayo). Census geocoded the address; aerial shows a low commercial building at the address consistent with a central office, but the building is not visually distinctive as a DC. Point on geocoded address.

Ark Data Centers (formerly Involta) Duluth 2, 421 N 6th Ave E, ~12,000 sqft. News confirms it is the former Daugherty Hardware building converted to a hardened DC (reinforced concrete, limited windows). Census geocoded to a specific downtown Duluth building; converted building is not visually distinguishable from neighbors in aerial. Point on geocoded address.

Network 222 (Fiore Companies), 222 W Washington Ave, 3MW colo inside a ten-story, ~190,000 sqft downtown Madison office building (near the WI State Capitol, visible in aerial). In-building colo; correct office tower identified via geocoded address. Point on geocoded address.

NOCIX Clay, 201 E 16th Ave, North Kansas City, ~24,000 sqft modified building at 16th & Clay, 1.5MW. Census geocoded the address into a dense industrial district of many look-alike flat-roof warehouses; a large flat-roof building sits at the address but cannot be uniquely confirmed as the DC from aerial. Point on geocoded address.

iConnect Billings Fiber Hotel resolves to Granite Tower, 222 N 32nd St Suite 301, Billings (PeeringDB fac/380). This is an in-building colo/carrier hotel inside a downtown office tower. Original record only had 'Granite Tower' as address; I geocoded the resolved street address (222 N 32nd St). Aerial shows downtown Billings office blocks; correct tower is the address building. Point on geocoded address.

CENTRA PWM1, 340 Cumberland Ave, Portland ME (PeeringDB fac/11838) - the primary carrier-neutral telecom hotel in Maine, home to NNENIX exchange, ~3MW. In-building colo inside a downtown Portland building. Census geocoded the address; a large flat-roofed building sits at the address. Point on geocoded address.

DataBank DFW3, 8375 Dominion Pkwy, Plano, ~144,000 sqft purpose-built (2018), 13.5MW, with attached DFW8 expansion, on a 16-acre campus. Census geocoded the address cleanly, but the campus sits in the Legacy office-park where DataBank DCs resemble plain office/flex buildings and power comes from an OFF-SITE Oncor substation, so no adjacent-substation or windowless-box signal is visible; geocode and prior CSV coord also differ by ~0.4km. Building not uniquely confirmable from aerial. Point on geocoded street address.

Binary.net 'The Vault', 134 S 13th St, downtown Lincoln - colo built inside a former Federal Reserve Bank vault (30-inch concrete walls, 30-ton vault door) within a larger downtown building, ~10,000 sqft raised floor, 1MW. In-building colo; correct downtown building identified via geocoded address. Point on geocoded address.

KDWEB San Jose is an in-building colocation/interconnection suite on the 18th floor of the office tower at 50 W San Fernando St, downtown San Jose (confirmed by DataCenterMap, Inflect, Newby Ventures listings). Census geocode landed on the correct downtown office block; satellite shows the multi-story office building at the address. Being an in-building colo in an identified multi-tenant tower, correct building identified but no standalone DC signals to fully verify.

Cogent Cleveland (CLE04) is an in-building colo inside the historic 21-story Keith Building at 1621 Euclid Ave in Playhouse Square (confirmed by DataCenterMap, Inflect, Cogent's own page). Geocode landed exactly on the Keith Building block downtown; satellite confirms a large downtown office tower at the address. In-building colo in an identified tower.

Prov.net/Alpha3 Cloud Providence 1 is a small 4,000 sqft in-building colo at 935 Westminster St, Providence (confirmed by DataCenterMap, datacenters.com). Geocode matched the exact address; satellite shows a small commercial building block in the Westminster St corridor. Identity and address confirmed; small in-building colo so no standalone DC signals visible.

Iron Mountain NJE-1, a purpose-built 830,000 sqft / 30MW data center on a 43-acre campus at 3003 Woodbridge Ave, Edison NJ. The census point-address geocode was wrong (landed in a residential block), so I relocated using the original dataset coords and wide imagery. Satellite unmistakably shows a massive windowless data-center building whose roof is almost entirely covered by a dense grid of solar panels, matching NJE-1's documented 18,000-module / 7.2MW rooftop solar array (largest of any NA data center). Building adjacent to highway, no competing look-alike. Point placed on the solar-covered rooftop centroid.

Encore Technologies MSB1 at 9333 N Springboro Pike, Miamisburg OH is a 73,632 sqft building (built 1980) operated as LexisNexis' dedicated data center (confirmed by DataCenterMap, LoopNet, DCD reporting on the Encore/4620 Wesley LLC purchase). Geocode landed on Springboro Pike; the nearest matching building is a campus structure on the west side of the road. Address and identity confirmed but the building reads as an office-style campus building with no distinctive windowless-box/rooftop-cooling DC signature at this resolution, so not fully verified.

Ethoplex 'Edge DC - Fond du Lac' record is internally inconsistent and cannot be pinned to a building. The dataset street address (W6720 Subway Rd, Friendship WI 54937) is in Adams County, ~50 miles NW of Fond du Lac; the facility name says Fond du Lac; and Ethoplex's only PeeringDB-listed data center is at N115W19150 Edison Dr, Germantown WI 53022. No public source gives a street address or identifiable building for a Fond du Lac edge node. Conflicting sources, no verifiable building -> unresolvable.

DQE Communications Allegheny Center Mall is a 17,287 sqft in-building colo at 100 South Commons, Pittsburgh (confirmed by Inflect, DataCenterJournal, Baxtel). Geocode matched the address and landed on the large Allegheny Center / Nova Place mixed-use complex on the North Side; satellite shows the multi-building complex. Correct complex identified; specific colo building within the large multi-building complex not individually distinguishable, so in-building CANDIDATE.

DataBank Orangeburg is a 34-acre campus at 2000 Corporate Drive that hosts BOTH LGA3 (110,000 sqft, opened May 2025) and LGA4 under the same address (confirmed by DataBank, DCD, datacenterHawk). Satellite of the campus is a mixed-date mosaic showing multiple large buildings, including one clearly still under construction plus a completed white flat-roof hall and a dark building with rooftop mechanical units. With two named facilities sharing one address and no public floor plan to distinguish LGA3 from LGA4/construction, the specific LGA3 building cannot be reliably isolated.

DataBank DEN1 (formerly Latisys 'Data 393', later Zayo zColo) is a 53,200 sqft data center at 393 Inverness Parkway, Englewood CO (confirmed by DataBank datasheet at that address, Baxtel, datacenterHawk). Geocode landed within the Inverness office park on/near Inverness Parkway; the site sits among numerous similar office-park buildings and reads as an office-style building rather than a distinctive windowless DC box, so the specific building is identified by address but not visually verifiable. Point placed on the building adjacent to the geocoded address.

Digital Realty 200 Paul Avenue (SFO10/SFR1) is the well-known San Francisco carrier hotel, a 482,000 sqft 5-story data center on a 7.35-acre multi-building site ~4 miles south of downtown (confirmed by Digital Realty, DataCenterMap, Baxtel). Census point-geocode landed just south of the complex; imagery to the north shows the large data-center buildings with rows of dedicated rooftop cooling/HVAC equipment arrays characteristic of a major data hall, no competing look-alike. Point moved onto the main data-center building's rooftop centroid.

Atomic Data 'Minneapolis Central' (MSP250) is an in-building colo at 250 S Marquette Ave in the downtown Minneapolis core (confirmed by PeeringDB, Inflect, datacenters.com). Geocode matched 250 Marquette Ave S and landed on the correct downtown high-rise block; satellite shows dense downtown office towers at the address. In-building colo in an identified tower, no standalone DC signals to fully verify.

EdgeConneX San Diego SDG01 is a small edge colo (retrofit, ~0.5MW) occupying Suite 150B at 5761 Copley Drive, Kearny Mesa, San Diego (confirmed by datacenterHawk, EdgeConneX San Diego datasheet, DataCenterMap). Geocode landed at 5761 Copley Dr among flex/industrial buildings; the suite is inside a commercial building rather than a standalone DC, so the specific building at the address is identified but no distinctive DC signature is visible. Point placed on the building nearest the geocoded suite address.

Identity: Quest Technology Management 'High Availability Business Center' (HABC), operator HQ at 9000 Foothills Blvd, Roseville CA (questsys.com; datacenters.com listing). Census geocode landed on a road intersection with wrong ZIP (95747); ArcGIS geocode (38.7905,-121.3170) landed on the correct corporate campus. Esri satellite shows a large corporate campus: an office building with wings plus a large central rectangular building. Data center is an in-building colo within this HABC building, not a standalone windowless DC, so CANDIDATE. Point moved off the road onto the main central building.

Identity: DC BLOX HSV1, 13,000 SF Tier-3 data center at 333 Diamond Drive NW, Huntsville AL (dcblox.com; datacentermap). Census geocode matched exact address. Esri satellite shows a compact white windowless box building with dark rooftop mechanical units and an adjacent fenced electrical/mechanical equipment yard on its south side - classic small purpose-built data-center signals. The much larger structure to the east is a distribution warehouse (too large, no DC signals). Point placed on the DC BLOX building rooftop centroid.

Identity: UPX Miami is listed at 16901 Collins Avenue, Unit 3601, Sunny Isles Beach FL (PeeringDB fac/6529; UPX US LLC). Unit 3601 is a suite inside a beachfront RESIDENTIAL high-rise condominium tower, not a data-center building. Census geocode landed on the beach/shoreline (interpolation error). Esri satellite shows a strip of luxury beachfront condo/hotel towers; there is no identifiable standalone data-center structure and no way to confirm which tower/unit hosts the small IX presence. No verifiable DC building -> coords left null.

Identity: Ark Data Centers (formerly Involta) Marion/Cedar Rapids, 19,000 SF, 5055 Rec Dr, Marion IA (arkdna.com; datacentermap). Census geocode matched the address and landed at a rural crossroads. Esri satellite shows several nondescript light-industrial/commercial buildings around the intersection (gable-roofed storage-type buildings to the north, small flat-roofed white commercial buildings to the southwest) but NONE display distinctive data-center signals (no rooftop cooling arrays, no substation, no security-fenced DC box). Cannot confidently isolate which structure is the 19,000 SF data center -> AMBIGUOUS. Best-guess coords placed on the nearest flat-roofed commercial building to the addressed point.

Identity: iM Data Centers Miami (IM Interconnect) occupies the 7th floor of the Digital Realty/DataBank carrier hotel at 36 NE 2nd Street, downtown Miami FL (Digital Realty MIA10; inflect.com). Census geocode matched the exact street address in the dense downtown Miami grid. This is a multi-tenant in-building colo (a carrier hotel with many operators) where the specific building is identifiable but the DC is a floor within it -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the 36 NE 2nd St building at the geocoded address.

Identity: BLDR-1919 corresponds to 1919 14th St, Boulder CO 80302 - 'The Colorado Building', the tallest office building in downtown Boulder (now Industrious coworking; wwreynolds.com). Census geocode matched the exact address. This is an in-building network/colo presence inside an identifiable downtown office tower rather than a standalone data center; no distinct DC building exists -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the office building at the geocoded corner (14th & Walnut).

Identity: Expedient Pittsburgh - Nova Place, a ~26,000 SF colo/cloud data center at 1 Allegheny Center, Pittsburgh PA (North Side), inside Nova Place - the former Allegheny Center Mall, now a large mixed-use complex (expedient.com; Post-Gazette). Census geocode ('1 Allegheny Sq') matched to the complex. Esri satellite shows the large multi-building Nova Place superblock. The data center is an in-building colo within this complex, so the correct complex is identified but exact standalone building isn't a distinct DC -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the Nova Place main building footprint.

Identity: Stream Data Centers DFW VII, a greenfield hyperscale campus (275,000 SF, 43 MW, ~23 acres) at 1813 Lookout Dr, Garland TX, with a documented 95 MW on-site Oncor substation and fully leased to a major cloud customer in 2023 (streamdatacenters.com; datacenterhawk). Esri satellite clearly shows two large windowless data-center buildings with massive dense rooftop cooling-unit arrays, a large on-site electrical substation (rows of transformers/switchgear) along the north edge, and an additional building under construction (expansion) - all matching the documented facility. Point placed on the western primary data-center building.

Identity: XO/Verizon '600 West 7th' data center occupies the 3rd floor of the 7-story West 7th Center (W7C) carrier hotel at 600 West 7th St, downtown Los Angeles CA - one of the best-connected buildings in LA after One Wilshire (west7center.com; datacentermap; PeeringDB fac/1004). Census geocode matched the exact address in the downtown LA financial district. In-building colo/carrier hotel where the specific building is identifiable -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the W7C building at the geocoded address.

Identity: INOC ALB1, a small ~4,000 SF colo/meet-me facility at 80 State Street, downtown Albany NY (inoc.net; datacentermap; PeeringDB fac/1291). Census geocode matched the exact address. This is a small in-building colo inside an identifiable downtown Albany office building rather than a standalone DC -> CANDIDATE. Point placed on the 80 State St building at the geocoded address.

Identity: Rochelle Technology Center at 910 Technology Parkway, Rochelle IL 61068, in the Rochelle Business & Technology Park; address is Follett Corporation, which operated a data center there (rmu.net; cityofrochelle.net). Census returned no match; ArcGIS geocoded 910 Technology Pkwy to 41.9406,-89.0337 (score 100). Esri satellite shows a corporate building at the addressed point plus a larger distribution-warehouse building just north. Purpose-built corporate data center is plausible but could not be cleanly distinguished from the adjacent distribution structure -> CANDIDATE, low confidence. Point placed on the corporate building at the geocoded address.

Identity: CyrusOne NYM5 Norwalk, 168,000 SF (Connecticut's largest DC, 16 MW), at 6 Norden Place (f/k/a 10 Norden Place), Norwalk CT (cyrusone.com; datacentermap). The address is on the former Norden Systems / Northrop Grumman defense-electronics campus (multi-building, originally flagged ambiguous). The tight geocode initially showed only residential; a wider Esri view revealed the large industrial complex. The 'f/k/a 10 Norden Place' renumbering resolves the specific building - the ArcGIS geocode for 10 Norden Pl (41.1108,-73.3927) lands on the dominant large windowless complex, which the satellite shows with two circular cooling structures, mechanical/electrical yards, and an adjacent rail siding (data-center signals). Point placed on that main building; CANDIDATE because the campus is subdivided among tenants.

BestNAP is operated by Bestline Communications at 1122 Colorado St, Suite 110, downtown Austin (carrier-neutral in-building carrier hotel, ~3MW, 24x7 doorman). It is a suite inside a downtown office building, not a standalone DC. Census geocode matched 1122 Colorado St exactly (rooftop of the address block, near the TX Capitol visible in imagery). Point placed on the address building; specific floor/suite cannot be distinguished from imagery.

QTS JCY1 is an in-building colo occupying floors 16-18 of a 19-story building at 95 Christopher Columbus Drive, Jersey City (120,000 sqft, 3MW). It is a suite inside a downtown high-rise, not a standalone DC. Census geocode matched the street address to the Grove St area (correct neighborhood) but interpolation landed a block off onto a low residential row; no single 19-story tower could be unambiguously matched in imagery. Point at geocoded address; low confidence on exact tower.

East Kentucky Network (Appalachian Wireless) HQ campus at 271 Technology Trail, Ivel KY, houses a 16,700 sqft raised-floor DC with transport to Chicago/Ashburn. Census could not geocode the address; located the campus visually off US-23 in Ivel. Satellite (0.0036 deg tile) shows a corporate campus with a distinctive circular entrance drive/roundabout in front of a dark-roof building, plus a larger white-roof building and industrial buildings. Point placed on the main HQ building with the roundabout entrance; DC is within the campus, exact building among the campus structures not certain.

CyrusOne CIN5 (Lebanon OH). The dataset address '401 Fujitec Drive' corresponds to Fujitec's (elevator company) Lebanon facility, while sources list CyrusOne CIN5's own address as 401 Kingsview Drive — an address discrepancy. Census geocoded 401 Fujitec Dr to an industrial park containing multiple large flat-roof buildings (a red/white-roofed factory NW, a tan office/flex building NE) with no clear DC signature distinguishable at either 0.005 or 0.003 deg zoom; 401 Kingsview Dr would not geocode. Cannot confidently assign the correct building. Coords left null.

Clackamas Education Service District operates a small ~3,000 sqft data center / colocation within its facility (the 'Sunnybrook' site) at 13455 SE 97th Ave, Clackamas OR. This is an in-building institutional DC, not a standalone structure. Census geocode matched the address exactly (45.4267,-122.5628), landing among an office building and adjacent apartment complexes off I-205. Point at the geocoded address building; specific room-level DC not distinguishable from imagery.

CyrusOne NYM7 at 155 Myers Corners Rd, Wappingers Falls NY (100,000 sqft, 37,000 sqft raised floor, up to 10MW). Census geocoded to Myers Corners Rd; the only large commercial building at the address sits just NE of the road. 0.003 deg tile shows a large flat-roof building with rooftop mechanical/cooling units and a big parking lot, connected to a second structure to the NE — consistent with the DC. Point placed on the central large building; minor ambiguity between the two connected structures keeps it CANDIDATE rather than VERIFIED.

SDN Communications data center at Mark Shlanta Technology Park, 5300 N La Mesa Dr, NW of Sioux Falls (purpose-built 2012, 12-inch concrete walls, colocation/DR). Census geocoded to N La Mesa Dr; DC building sits just east. 0.003 deg tile clearly shows a single purpose-built DC: large dark flat-roof main hall with an attached lighter-roof section carrying rooftop cooling units, plus a distinctive circular array of tanks (fuel/water) to the NE. Rural site, no look-alike buildings. Point on the building's rooftop centroid.

DataBank MSP3 at 8111 Oxbow Creek Dr N, Brooklyn Park MN (opened 2021, 82,231 sqft, 9MW, 14-acre campus with room to expand). Address would not geocode via Census; located on the campus visually. 0.0032 deg tile shows a purpose-built white-roof DC with an exterior row of generator/cooling units along the south side and a large empty gravel expansion pad immediately east — matching the described 14-acre expandable campus. Point on the building rooftop centroid.

Evocative LAX14 (formerly Internap LAX014) at 3690 Redondo Beach Ave, Redondo Beach CA (one-story structural steel/concrete DC built 2012, 104,000 sqft, 7MW). Census geocoded to 3690 Redondo Beach Ave. 0.003 deg tile shows a one-story building whose roof carries a dense array of rooftop cooling/HVAC units — a classic data-center signature — matching the described single-story facility. Point on the rooftop centroid of the cooling-unit building.

ColoCenter.com Manchester (now FirstLight Fiber) is an in-building colo on the 2nd floor at 77 Sundial Avenue, Manchester NH (14,400 sqft, 4MW). Census geocode matched 77 Sundial Ave. Imagery shows a large beige commercial building with a big parking lot in the Sundial Ave business park — the address building. It is a suite inside that building, not a standalone DC. Point placed on the large building; specific floor/suite not distinguishable.

Cologix DSM1 is a carrier-hotel colo located inside the Financial Center building at 666 Walnut Street, downtown Des Moines (4,000 sqft meet-me-room, DesMoinesIX). It is a suite in a downtown office high-rise, not a standalone DC. Census geocode matched 666 Walnut St exactly. Point placed on the Financial Center building; specific floor/suite not distinguishable from imagery.

Quad State Internet PAH1 is a small ISP colocation / light-colo at 1212 Helen St, Paducah KY (carrier-neutral, hosts a Paducah IX and a Hurricane Electric PoP). Census geocoded 1212 Helen St, but the surrounding area is a residential/mixed-use neighborhood; the geocoded point falls on trees/a vacant parcel with only small nondescript houses and a couple of small commercial/garage buildings nearby. No data-center building signature is visible and the exact small building housing the colo cannot be distinguished. Coords left null.

FirstLight 'Regen Hut' (PeeringDB fac/3860) is a long-haul fiber regeneration/interconnection hut in the Portland ME metro, not a conventional data-center building. Address 9 Westland Ave is unconfirmed by any source and the CSV zip (04401 Bangor) conflicts with the geocoded result (Portland 04102). Satellite of the geocoded point shows a residential neighborhood with a retail plaza to the east and no identifiable telecom/DC structure. A regen hut is a tiny unmanned box that cannot be resolved to a specific rooftop from imagery.

DataBank IND1 is a 54,000 sqft downtown carrier hotel at 731 W Henry St in the telecom-dense Henry Street area (databank.com confirms address). Census geocode lands squarely on a large flat dark-roofed industrial building in a riverside cluster of similar buildings. It is an in-building urban colo; the addressed building is identified but lacks a distinctive standalone DC signature and sits among look-alike industrial structures, hence CANDIDATE.

DartPoints BTR1 (7127 Florida Blvd) is inside the Bon Carre Technology Park, a former shopping-mall complex (86,400 sqft, confirmed by datacenterHawk/DCD). Geocode landed on Florida Blvd; moved the point north onto the large white-roofed Bon Carre complex clearly visible at top of image (distinct mall footprint with courtyard/tree pattern). Co-located in the same building as BTR2 (dc_b15cee237812); the specific suite cannot be distinguished from imagery, so CANDIDATE at building level.

LADC4 (Colocation America) is a colo suite on the 4th floor of the 'Telecom Center LA' office building at 530 W 6th St, adjacent to One Wilshire (datacenterjournal/inflect confirm building and floor). Census geocode lands on the correct downtown high-rise footprint. In-building colo where the host tower is identified but the DC is not a standalone structure, hence CANDIDATE.

EdgeConneX Portland EDCPOR01 is 'Building C, Suite C-300' at 6327 NE/NW Evergreen Parkway, Hillsboro, part of a multi-building corporate park also hosting EDCPOR02 (datacenterHawk). Census geocode landed on the Evergreen Parkway road/intersection, not on a building. Imagery shows several near-identical office/flex buildings north of the intersection with no way to identify which is Building C. Multiple candidate buildings, so AMBIGUOUS.

Southern Telecom (Southern Company subsidiary) operates a 5,400 sqft carrier-neutral colo at 270 Peachtree Street NE, downtown Atlanta (MetroAtlantaCEO/Southern Company press confirm street; the CSV 'W Peachtree' is an error, correct street is Peachtree St NE). Census geocode lands on the correct 270 Peachtree office tower footprint. Small in-building colo suite within a downtown tower, so CANDIDATE.

Digital Realty DFW11 (4025 Midway Rd) is a single-story 100,600 sqft building within the ~69-acre 'Digital Dallas' campus. datacentermap publishes coordinate 33.021668,-96.844732; that point lands on a large single-story building on the west side of Midway Rd showing rows of rooftop mechanical units and circular rooftop cooling/screening features consistent with a DC. My independently estimated rooftop centroid (33.021665,-96.844728) matches the published coordinate. Multi-building campus so CANDIDATE.

EdgeConneX Denver EDCDEN01 is a small 9,800 sqft colo in 'Building 3, Suite 500/600' at 8535 Highfield Parkway, Englewood (datacenterHawk). Census geocode for 8535 Highfield lands on an office/flex building at image center within the Meridian business park. Small suite in a multi-building park; the addressed building is on the point but the DC has no distinct standalone signature, so CANDIDATE with lower confidence.

365 Data Centers Aurora CO1 (3431 N Windsor Dr) is a purpose-built 150,000 sqft data center, 8MW (formerly SunGard). Imagery just west of the geocoded address shows a very large windowless box with a bright white membrane roof, rooftop mechanical units, perimeter fencing and a fenced yard on the west side, and a large dedicated parking lot, matching a purpose-built DC. Placed point on that building's rooftop centroid. Not VERIFIED because the surrounding warehouse district has similar large white-roofed boxes.

IP Pathways DSM1 (3600 109th St, Urbandale) has a 37,135 sqft building footprint (datacentermap/inflect). Census geocode lands in a suburban office/flex park; placed the point on the flat-roofed building adjacent to the geocode. No strong standalone DC signature (no visible cooling array or substation) and several similar buildings nearby, so CANDIDATE with low confidence.

DartPoints Columbus CLU1 (2425 Technology Blvd, formerly 'Data Cave') is a purpose-built 86,400 sqft EF-5-rated data center (dartpoints.com/datacentermap). Imagery at the geocoded address shows two adjacent industrial buildings in a semi-rural tech park; placed the point on the larger light-gray flat-roofed building consistent with the 86k sqft footprint. Two candidate buildings and a winter image without clear rooftop cooling, so CANDIDATE.

DartPoints BTR2 (7139 Florida Blvd) is in the same Bon Carre Technology Park former-mall complex as BTR1 (dc_ab2a3e5edefd); addresses 7127 and 7139 are entrances to the same building. Geocode landed on Florida Blvd; moved the point onto the Bon Carre complex (top-right of image, distinctive mall footprint). BTR1 and BTR2 share one building and cannot be distinguished at suite level from imagery, so CANDIDATE at building level.

CENTRA CLT01 (formerly Deep Edge Realty) is an interconnection/colocation facility inside the building at 701 E Trade St, Charlotte NC, confirmed by PeeringDB (fac/7878), datacentermap and inflect listings. Census geocoded 701 E Trade St to the intersection at 35.2221,-80.8370; satellite shows a large flat-roof building with rooftop mechanical units immediately east of that intersection. In-building colo, so point placed on that building's roof rather than a standalone DC box.

Colo Atl / 'Colo at 55, LLC' operates on the 5th and 8th floors of the 55 Marietta St NW carrier hotel, a 403,000 sqft 21-story tower (recently acquired by RadiusDC per DCD/PRNewswire). In-building colo in a well-documented full-block carrier hotel; point on the tower footprint.

ARE-ON Hope is a tiny 100 kW / 30-ton research-network node (PeeringDB fac/1669) at 358 Hwy 174 N, Hope AR. Satellite of both locations shows no identifiable data-center signature (no windowless box, cooling array, substation, or fencing); a 100 kW node is likely a small room inside an existing structure. No way to disambiguate the two candidate locations or pinpoint a building, so coords left null.

Equinix AT1 occupies floors 2/3/6 of the 6-story building at 180 Peachtree St NW, Atlanta (Equinix official page, datacenterhawk). 79,200 sqft total. Census geocoded 180 Peachtree St to 33.7585,-84.3877 in the downtown Atlanta highrise core; point sits on that building block. In-building colo, correct building identified by address.

DataBank ATL3 (Atlanta West End) is a dedicated 75,000 sqft standalone data-center building at 1150 White St SW (DataBank official page, datacenterhawk). Point moved onto that building's rooftop centroid. Dedicated DC building clearly seen, no competing look-alike.

BlueBridge Networks Cleveland colo is on the 5th floor, Suite 500 of 1255 Euclid Ave, Cleveland OH (inflect, datacentermap; hosts regional 911 response center). Census geocoded 1255 Euclid Ave to 41.5007,-81.6837 on the downtown Cleveland building block. In-building colo, correct building identified by address.

Netrality's 401 N Broad St is the 1.3 million sqft Terminal Commerce Building carrier hotel, the most interconnected facility in Pennsylvania (Netrality official page, datacenterfrontier). Census geocoded 401 N Broad St to 39.9596,-75.1619; point sits on that full-block building in Philadelphia's Callowhill district. In-building colo in a well-documented landmark carrier hotel.

3HCloud is listed as a network/peer at the 365 Main St, San Francisco facility (Digital Realty SFO12/365 Main, an 8.6 MW carrier-neutral DC in a former steel-and-concrete tank assembly plant; datacentermap, peeringdb). Dataset coords 38.94927,-77.55563 (Arcola, Loudoun County VA) land squarely on a large windowless data-center building: satellite shows a long rectangular white-roof structure with regularly-spaced rooftop cooling units, adjacent construction of more DC buildings, and retention ponds - unmistakable hyperscale DC signature. Building type verified from imagery; point placed on the DC building. Operator identity is ambiguous ('Arcola Tech Park' is a generic name; the Arcola area hosts Google, Yondr, PowerHouse and St. John Properties campuses), hence CANDIDATE not VERIFIED.

Psychz Networks LAX1 is a 15,000 sqft carrier-neutral colo in Suite 200 of 700 Wilshire Blvd, Los Angeles (inflect, datacentermap, peeringdb fac/2185). Heyburn Building is a historic 17-story tower at 4th & Broadway (332 W Broadway), Louisville, housing in-building colo/interconnection (Cogent, Lumen/tw telecom on the 17th floor). In-building colo in an office tower, so no rooftop-DC signals; identity firm from address + named landmark. Point on the geocoded building.

Fiber Alley operates edge data centers at 8915/8917 Complex Dr in the Kearny Mesa business park, San Diego. Census geocode matched 8915 Complex Dr and lands among a row of nearly identical flat-roofed light-industrial/commercial buildings. No distinctive DC signal (cooling array/substation) visible; the specific edge-DC suite cannot be uniquely picked out from look-alike neighbors. Point placed on the flat-roofed building fronting Complex Dr nearest the geocode.

'Hub Express System' (HEX) is not a data center building: it is TOWARDEX/TWDX Infrastructure's open-access underground fiber conduit and vault system (a 'Joint Use Communications Trench') running ~1 km beneath the streets of Somerville's Inner Belt, with rectangular concrete vaults and 24-30x 4in conduit banks. The listing address '0 Inner Belt Rd' is a placeholder for linear underground infrastructure. There is no discrete building to place a point on -> UNRESOLVABLE.

Flexential SLC05 (former ViaWest) is at 6340 S 3000 E Ste 510, Cottonwood Heights. Census geocode landed at the 3000 E / ~6200 S road intersection. Surrounding imagery is a mix of a large retail/mall structure (NW), garden-style apartment complexes (E, with pools), and small commercial buildings (NE, SW); none shows an unambiguous data-center signature and the exact building cannot be confidently distinguished among the several candidates. Leaving coords null.

DartPoints Spartanburg SPA1 is described as a purpose-built stand-alone facility (~21,400 sqft) at 5700 N Blackstock Rd. Census geocode matched the address; satellite (zoomed) shows a single white flat-roofed windowless building with small rooftop mechanical units, its own perimeter parking, and a large ground-mounted solar array immediately adjacent to the SE - a strong DC signature with no competing look-alike. Point placed on the building rooftop centroid.

Digital Realty LAX12 is a 2-story, ~132,000 sqft dedicated data center at 2260 E El Segundo Blvd, El Segundo. Census geocode matched the address (SE of the El Segundo Blvd intersection). Zoomed satellite shows a large gray windowless building with a visible row of rooftop cooling units directly at that corner - matching a dedicated DC. Original 'no_building_found_manual' status upgraded; point moved off the road onto the building rooftop.

LCOG Park Place is a carrier-neutral interconnection facility (carrier hotel) operated by the Lane Council of Governments in the basement and 2nd floor of the Park Place building at 859 Willamette St, downtown Eugene (LCOG's own offices; ~20-23 cabinets). Census geocode matched the address on the correct downtown block. It is explicitly an in-building interconnection room, not a standalone server hall, so no external DC signals; identity firm from address + operator page. Point on the geocoded building.

Fireline Broadband runs a small (~1 MW, 100-ton) NOC/data center at 9911 Norwalk Blvd, Santa Fe Springs. Census geocode matched the address at a Norwalk Blvd intersection surrounded by near-identical commercial/industrial buildings on all corners. No distinctive DC signature; the exact single-tenant building could not be visually confirmed. Point placed on the commercial building at the address frontage; low confidence.

200 Quannapowitt Pkwy, Wakefield is a ~218,000 sqft former Digital Realty data center (now AXCELX colo, 24 MW). Census geocode matched 200 Quannapowitt and lands on a dark-roofed office-style building with multiple white rooftop HVAC units by Lake Quannapowitt. Building is DC-consistent but presents as an office building and sits beside a near-identical look-alike (100 Quannapowitt / former Comverse complex), so the exact building is not unambiguous. Point on the geocoded building.

Alpheus Austin (now LOGIX Fiber Networks) is a small in-building colo (~12,000 gross / 3,000 usable sqft, Suite 150, 2.4 MW) at 1905 E 6th St, East Austin. Census geocode matched the address on the correct block. It is a suite within a building, so no standalone DC signals; identity firm from exact address + operator listing. Point on the geocoded building.

Telehouse New York Teleport is at 7 Teleport Drive within The Teleport office park, Staten Island. Census geocode matched 7 Teleport Dr; the point sat in open ground, so it was moved WSW onto the actual building. Zoomed satellite shows a large windowless flat-roofed building in an isolated, fully-fenced secure compound with rows of rooftop mechanical units and a dedicated parking lot set apart in woodland - an unmistakable data-center signature with no competing structure. Point on the building rooftop centroid.

Level(3)/Lumen Atlanta Peachtree is a carrier-neutral in-building colocation on the 3rd/4th floors of the office tower at 180 Peachtree St NW, downtown Atlanta (datacentermap, inflect). Census geocode of 180 Peachtree St NW lands on the tower's street frontage in a dense downtown grid; correct building/tower identified by address. Not a standalone DC so no rooftop cooling signature to confirm; point placed on the geocoded building.

Ethoplex Edge DC Green Bay at claimed 4151 Heritage Rd, De Pere WI could not be confirmed. Ethoplex's documented facilities are in Germantown/SE Wisconsin, not De Pere (ethoplex.com, peeringdb). Census geocode of the address lands on rural farmland/quarry with no data-center building; the dataset's suggested coords ~2.5km away land inside a single-family residential subdivision. No identifiable DC building at either location; small edge micro-DC with no public building info. Coords left null.

QTS Atlanta-Suwanee (SUW1) at 300 Satellite Blvd is a 370,000 sqft tilt-up concrete data center sited in a wooded valley (datacenters.com, datacentermap). Zoomed Esri imagery on the parcel NE of the geocoded intersection shows a single very large windowless light-concrete building with rooftop cooling-unit arrays and small circular tanks, surrounded by trees, matching the described building. Census geocode landed on the entrance road/intersection; point moved onto the building's rooftop centroid.

DQE Communications Downtown Pittsburgh is a small (~3,000 sqft, 1.2 MW) in-building colocation at 322 4th Ave, Pittsburgh (datacentermap, peeringdb fac/8125). Census geocode places the point on the 4th Ave building in the dense downtown core; correct building identified by address. In-building colo with no standalone DC signature to confirm.

Colo@34 (Performive/CloudFirst) is an in-building colocation at 34 Peachtree St, downtown Atlanta, a bandwidth-rich carrier location near the downtown Telx POP (datacenterjournal, datacentermap). Census geocode lands on the 34 Peachtree building frontage in the dense downtown grid; correct building identified by address. In-building colo, no standalone rooftop signature.

H5 Data Centers Tampa (TA1) is a 27,300 sqft in-building colo occupying Suite 1000 of the Franklin Exchange building, a distinctive downtown Tampa high-rise (once the city's tallest), at 655 N Franklin St (h5datacenters.com, datacenterhawk). Census geocode places the point on the Franklin Exchange tower; correct building/tower identified by address. In-building colo, no external DC signature.

Mainstream Technologies Little Rock data center occupies the 2nd floor of the former Federal Reserve Building at 325 W Capitol Ave, downtown Little Rock (concrete-steel structure) per datacentermap and cloudandcolocation. Census geocode places the point on the 325 W Capitol Ave building; correct building identified by address. In-building colo, no standalone rooftop signature.

CENTRA CMH01 (formerly Deep Edge) is a 2 MW in-building interconnection colo on the B1 level of the Borden Building, a 438-ft skyscraper at 180 E Broad St, Columbus OH (baxtel, datacentermap, peeringdb fac/12210). Census geocode places the point on the Borden Building tower; correct building identified by address. (Dataset zip 40101 was wrong; street address correct.) In-building colo, no external DC signature.

Interphase Data Center New York II: no public street address available. Dataset address '85th Avenue, New York NY 10011' is invalid (10011 is Chelsea/Manhattan; 85th Ave is in Queens) and Census geocoder returned NO MATCH. Sources (newby-ventures) confirm it is a NYC interconnection facility but publish no street address. No location or building could be established; coords left null.

Iron Mountain VA-2 is a 221,578 sqft purpose-built DC at 11660 Hayden Rd on Iron Mountain's 142-acre, ~5-building (expanding to 9) Manassas campus (ironmountain.com, datacenterhawk). Zoomed Esri imagery shows large windowless data-center buildings with dense rooftop cooling-unit arrays on the east side; the Census geocode landed on vacant construction land between the campus and adjacent townhomes, so the point was moved onto the nearest large DC building. Confirmed as an Iron Mountain DC building, but because the campus holds multiple near-identical buildings, exact VA-2 identity vs a sibling is not fully certain.

Pixel Factory Data Center is a small colo (150 kVA UPS, single 20-ton HVAC) at 9680 Atlee Commons Dr, Ashland VA, in a flex/office park off I-95 (pixelfactorydc.com, datacentermap). Census geocode landed on a vacant grass lot; imagery shows a two-story office building with parking adjacent to the NW, plus surrounding flex/industrial units. No distinctive DC signature is visible (expected for a micro-colo). Point placed on the adjacent office building as the likely address building; low confidence on exact unit.

Equinix LA4 (paired with LA3) is a single-story tilt-up concrete 177,469 sqft data center at 445 N Douglas St, El Segundo (equinix.com, datacentermap, datacenterhawk). Census geocode landed on Douglas St; zoomed imagery shows a large flat-roofed single-story industrial building on the west side of Douglas matching the described structure, versus corporate offices with landscaping on the east side. Point moved onto that west-side building; medium confidence given a couple of large candidate footprints in the block.

Equinix PH1 is a carrier-neutral in-building colo (Suite 990, ~26,780 sqft) inside the 11-story commercial building at 401 N Broad Street, downtown Philadelphia. Census geocoded the street address onto the correct large building block on Broad Street. As an in-building colo in a multi-tenant tower there is no distinct windowless-DC signature visible from satellite; the correct building is identified by well-documented address. Point placed on the addressed building.

NP Rochester is a small PeeringDB-listed interconnection/colo facility at 220 S Broadway, downtown Rochester MN. Census geocoded to the addressed building in the downtown grid (river to the east in image). No distinct data-center building signature visible from satellite (address-level in-building colo). Point on the geocoded building; low confidence on exact suite.

Wnet Telecom USA is a small PeeringDB colo listed at 8760 23rd Ave, Suite 2, Brooklyn (Bath Beach/Bensonhurst). Satellite shows dense low-rise residential/mixed Brooklyn blocks with no visible data-center building signature; this is a small in-building/suite colo. Census geocoded the specific address onto a low-rise building. Point on that building, low confidence, no DC signal observable.

MDC1 (Colocation America, also DataBank/Digital Realty MIA10) is a ~12,000 sqft in-building colo at 36 NE 2nd St, Suite 400, downtown Miami, near the NAP of the Americas. Census geocoded to the correct downtown building. In-building colo in a multi-tenant tower so no standalone DC box visible; correct building identified by address. Point on the addressed building.

TierPoint Hawthorne (formerly XAND Westchester), 11 Skyline Drive, 46,000 sqft. Operator docs state TierPoint runs TWO separate buildings on a shared 91-acre campus with Westchester Medical Center. Satellite shows an office-park cluster with no unambiguous windowless-DC box; both TierPoint buildings share the campus so identity is correct but which of the two is ambiguous. Point placed on the addressed building near the geocode.

21715 Filigree Court, Ashburn is a data-center campus (also Equinix DC2 and INAP WDC002 'Bldg F' at the same address). Satellite clearly shows large data-center buildings with characteristic rows of rooftop cooling units (confirmed on tight crop). Census geocode landed on the internal roundabout/road; I moved the point west onto the clearest DC building. RC Solutions is a colo tenant on this multi-building lettered campus so the exact building is somewhat ambiguous, hence low confidence.

Metanet Chelsea is a ~60,000 sqft in-building colo at 85 Tenth Avenue, Manhattan (Chelsea, one block from the Hudson, visible in image). 85 Tenth Ave is a large full-block telecom/office building. Census geocoded the address onto the correct building block. In-building colo so no standalone DC signature; correct building identified by address. Point on the addressed building.

IC2NET is a ~2.5 MW in-building colo at 14351 Myford Rd, Tustin, in a large business/industrial park. Satellite shows a park of similar large flat-roof commercial buildings with no distinctive DC signature; census geocode landed on a landscaped median/road. Point moved onto the large addressed building nearest the geocode. Low confidence: generic business-park building, colo inside, exact building hard to distinguish from neighbors.

Data Holdings Milwaukee, 3135 W Highland Blvd, is a purpose-built two-story hardened Tier III+ data center (46,000 sqft, 8 MW). Tight satellite crop clearly shows a distinctive white two-story building on the south side of Highland Blvd with a full array of rooftop mechanical/cooling units and rooftop tanks - a textbook DC roof. Matches operator description exactly, no competing look-alike. Census geocode was on the adjacent boulevard; point moved onto the building's rooftop centroid.

Venyu BTR1 (now DartPoints), 7127 Florida Blvd, is a 34,000 sqft DC floor within the ~47,000 sqft Bon Carre Business Center (a large former-mall complex) in Baton Rouge. Satellite shows Florida Blvd across the middle with the large Bon Carre complex on the north side. In-building colo within a very large multi-use complex so the exact DC section cannot be isolated from satellite; census geocode was on Florida Blvd, point moved north onto the Bon Carre building. Correct complex identified, low confidence on exact unit.

Fusion Connect Cleveland (fka Fidelity Voice and Data), 1621 Euclid Ave, is an in-building colo inside the historic 21-story Keith Building at Playhouse Square, downtown Cleveland (within an H5 data-center building). Census geocoded the address to the correct downtown building on Euclid Ave. Named landmark building identified; in-building colo so no standalone DC signature visible. Point on the addressed building.

Cloudpath CT19, 5 Landmark Square, Stamford CT, is a 450 kW in-building colo within the Landmark Square office complex (Cloudpath's own contact is 6 Landmark Square, next door). Census failed to match the address; satellite fetched at the PeeringDB coordinates shows a downtown Stamford office complex at center. In-building colo within a multi-building office complex so exact building is somewhat ambiguous; point on the central Landmark Square building. Low confidence.

DNSnetworks IDC4 is an in-building colocation/interconnection facility at 36 NE 2nd St, a ~160,000 sqft downtown Miami carrier hotel (confirmed via PeeringDB fac/9707 and datacentermap). Census geocoded 36 NE 2nd St to 25.776033,-80.193092, landing on the correct downtown block. Satellite shows dense downtown mid-rise buildings; carrier-hotel colos are not visually distinguishable from offices, but the correct address building is identified. Point set on the geocoded address building.

MDC Nogales (NOG1) is a small carrier-neutral colo (0.2-1MW) at 81 N Sonoita Ave, confirmed via MDC Data Centers site and datacentermap. Census geocoded the address cleanly to 31.335472,-110.943007. Satellite shows small commercial buildings and parking at that point; no distinct windowless DC box or cooling array visible for such a small in-building colo, but the correct street address is identified. Point on geocoded address.

DataBank SLC1 is the primary Salt Lake City carrier hotel, a multi-tenant colo (Suite 200, 11,000 sqft) at 179 Social Hall Ave, confirmed via databank.com facility page. Census geocoded to 40.768287,-111.885994 on the correct downtown SLC block. Satellite shows downtown multi-tenant buildings; in-building colo not visually distinguishable but correct address building identified. Point on geocoded address.

XMission Salt Lake City is a carrier-neutral in-building colo (hosts the SLIX exchange) at 67 E 400 S, confirmed via PeeringDB fac/1335 and inflect. Census geocoded 67 E 400 S to 40.760709,-111.889029 on the correct downtown block. Satellite shows downtown SLC buildings near the state capitol/library area; in-building colo, correct address identified. Point on geocoded address.

Atlantech Online is an 8,000 sqft Tier-2 in-building colo occupying space in a multi-tenant building at 1010 Wayne Ave, downtown Silver Spring, operating there since 1998 (confirmed via datacentermap and atlantech.net). Census geocoded 1010 Wayne Ave to 38.994508,-77.027147 on the correct downtown block. In-building colo, correct address building identified. Point on geocoded address.

AUBix Auburn is a purpose-built 40,000 sqft standalone data center on a 6-acre site at 1571 W Samford Ave (confirmed via datacentermap, aubix.net, AL Governor grand-opening release; now leased by Core Scientific). Census geocoded the E-Samford variant wrongly (~5km east), so used dataset suggested coords. Satellite zoom clearly shows a windowless white box building with rooftop mechanical/cooling units, a fenced compound, and an adjacent darker electrical/generator yard, surrounded by trees on its own parcel. Point moved onto the building rooftop centroid.

H5 Data Centers Buffalo II is a 409,200 sqft Tier III data center on 43 acres in Lockport NY, the former Yahoo! Lockport facility (confirmed via datacentermap and H5 site). Census geocoded 5369 Crown Dr to 43.169929,-78.748651. Satellite zoom shows the unmistakable long, narrow parallel 'chicken-coop' free-air-cooling buildings characteristic of the Yahoo Lockport design, on a large fenced campus with substation/parking. Point moved onto the main building cluster centroid.

Foremost Corpus Christi is a carrier interconnection point (PeeringDB fac/12069, CLLI CRCHTX93W48) located in Suite 822 of the downtown office tower at 606 N Carancahua St. Census geocoded the address to 27.795751,-97.397393 on the correct downtown block. Satellite shows downtown Corpus Christi high-rise towers; as an 8th-floor in-building suite the correct tower is identified but no external DC signal is visible. Point on geocoded address.

Gafachi operates a ~24,499 sqft, 3MW colo at 1 W Main St, downtown Rochester (confirmed via DataCenterJournal and inflect building page). Census geocoded '1 Main St W' to 43.155657,-77.612519 near the Main St bridge over the Genesee River. Satellite shows downtown Rochester buildings at that corner; in-building colo, correct address building identified. Point on geocoded address.

Level(3)/Lumen Herndon 1 is at 520 Van Buren St, but Lumen operates an adjacent sibling data center (Herndon 3 at 524 Van Buren St) on the same street per datacentermap. Census geocoded 520 Van Buren to 38.964798,-77.382891 which lands on a road/intersection in an office park. Satellite shows several nondescript flat-roofed office-park buildings that cannot be reliably distinguished as 520 vs 524, and the geocode is interpolated to a road. Cannot confidently pick the exact building; coords left null.

Iron Mountain KCM-1 (Suite 817, 6301 Winchester Ave) is an underground data center located 110 feet beneath the surface in the Winchester limestone-mine business center (confirmed via ironmountain.com and datacentermap). Census geocoded the surface address to 39.010831,-94.504569, landing on wooded/graded terrain with surrounding office buildings. There is no surface data-center building to place a point on; the data halls are subterranean. Coords left null.

Evocative San Jose SV7/SJC7 is a ~21,000-35,000 sqft colo at 534 Stockton Ave, in the industrial zone beside the Diridon/railyard (confirmed via datacentermap, PeeringDB fac/147). Census geocoded 534 Stockton Ave to 37.338295,-121.910745. Satellite zoom shows a large windowless flat-roofed box building at the address with an adjacent fenced yard, consistent with a colo; rooftop cooling/substation signals not distinctly resolved. Point moved onto that building's rooftop centroid.

Fireline Broadband LA1 is a carrier-neutral colocation suite inside the 5900 Wilshire Blvd office tower, Los Angeles (confirmed by PeeringDB fac/1227, Inflect and firelinebroadband.com). Census geocode of 5900 Wilshire Blvd resolves exactly to the address. Satellite shows the dense urban Miracle Mile block south of the La Brea Tar Pits; the colo occupies a suite in the multi-tenant office tower rather than a standalone DC box, so CANDIDATE with point on the addressed building.

Newport Financial Center = 111 Town Square Place / 111 Pavonia Ave carrier hotel developed by LeFrak in Jersey City (datacentermap, inflect). Census geocode matches 111 Pavonia Ave exactly. Satellite shows the dense Newport high-rise cluster beside the Hudson River. It is an in-building colo within a LeFrak office tower (rooftop N+1 generators noted in listings) among several similar towers, so CANDIDATE at the addressed building.

OCDC = Orange County's data center at 1400 S Grand Ave, Santa Ana, operated in the county-owned building (PeeringDB fac/378; OC government amendment PDF confirms County owns 1400 South Grand Ave used as the Orange County Data Center; ~15,748 sqft). Census geocode resolves to the address but lands at the S Grand Ave intersection; the colo occupies a suite in a commercial building, so CANDIDATE at the addressed parcel.

Meta Los Lunas campus at 4250 Messenger Loop NW (Meta's own site + DCD). Satellite at the dataset coordinate shows the entire frame filled with unmistakable hyperscale DC buildings: multiple long rectangular white-roofed halls with rooftop cooling arrays, loading docks and service drives in the NM desert. Single-operator campus and the point sits directly on a data-center hall, so VERIFIED.

ManagedWay SFJ2 at 24245/24275 Northwestern Hwy, Southfield MI (PeeringDB fac/3502, inflect; 1.5 MW). Satellite southeast of the M-10 Lodge Freeway shows a single low-rise building with a dense array of white rooftop cooling/CRAC units (strong DC signature) and adjacent parking. Point moved onto that building's roof. In-building/single low-rise colo so CANDIDATE.

H5 Data Centers NY occupies 325 Hudson St, a 10-story 240,000 sqft telecom hotel (former industrial building, telecom-hotel since 1998) in Hudson Square (H5 site, datacenterhawk). Census geocode matches 325 Hudson St. Satellite shows dense lower-Manhattan blocks; carrier-hotel colo within the multi-tenant telecom building, so CANDIDATE at the addressed building.

CenterSquare (Csquare) IAD3-A confirmed at 22995 Wilder Court, Sterling VA, a single-story purpose-built DC (PeeringDB fac/2618, datacenterhawk). Census geocode landed on the Wilder Court roadway inside a Dulles flex/warehouse park. Satellite shows many near-identical tan/dark flex buildings with truck loading docks and no clearly distinguishing DC signature (no visible substation or rooftop CRAC array) attributable to one specific building, so the exact building cannot be disambiguated from imagery: AMBIGUOUS.

Equinix MI1 = NAP of the Americas (NOTA), a six-story 750,000 sqft hardened DC at 50 NE 9th St, downtown Miami (Wikipedia, Equinix, datacenterhawk). Tight satellite crop clearly shows the distinctive full-block building with two prominent white rooftop radomes and heavy rooftop equipment matching the iconic hardened facility. Point placed on that building: VERIFIED.

DC BLOX ATL1 at 6 West Druid Hills Dr NE, Atlanta (Brookhaven, near I-85/Buford Hwy) is a small ~3,350 sqft colo suite with two 1MW generators (PeeringDB fac/7220, datacenterhawk). Census geocode matches the address; satellite shows a commercial building at a highway-interchange block. Small in-building colo, no standalone DC box visible, so CANDIDATE at the addressed building.

DC BLOX MYR1 Myrtle Beach Cable Landing Station, 1401 Howard Ave in the ITAP park, purpose-built precast concrete (datacentermap, DC BLOX). Satellite clearly shows a standalone cross/plus-shaped large white windowless building with an adjacent equipment yard, rooftop units and a security drive loop, isolated in greenfield land, no competing look-alike. Point moved onto the building centroid: VERIFIED.

5Nines Data at 222 W Washington Ave, Madison WI, housed in the 'Network222' class-A office building owned by Fiore Companies (PeeringDB fac/559, inflect, 5nines.com). Census geocode matches the address; satellite shows a downtown Madison office block near the State Capitol (visible cross-shaped Capitol upper-right). Colo within a multi-tenant office building, so CANDIDATE at the addressed building.

Dataset row is only 'Google Data Center', AZ, with no street address. The dataset coordinate (33.3541,-111.6764) lands on plowed farmland with no building. Google's Mesa campus is reported at the Elliot Rd/Sossaman Rd corridor (datacenters.com, DCD), but imagery of that corridor shows multiple large hyperscale-style DC buildings (Google, Meta Mesa and others) with no marking to attribute a specific building to Google. Cannot confidently place the exact Google building: UNRESOLVABLE, coords left null.

Identity confirmed: 180 E Broad St is the Borden Building / Continental Plaza, a 438-ft downtown Columbus skyscraper hosting a carrier-neutral Meet-Me-Room and multiple colo operators (CENTRA/CMH01, Windstream, US Signal, Bresco). This is an in-building colocation/MMR, not a standalone data center. Census geocode is address-precise and lands on the tower at image center. No distinct data-center rooftop signature visible (urban high-rise, colo occupies interior floors), so CANDIDATE: correct building identified by well-documented address, in-building colo.

Identity confirmed: NYI NY1 at 100 William Street, a 21-story Financial District tower (black slate facade, built 1974) housing a carrier-neutral colo with ~30,000 sqft on interior floors. In-building colocation, not a standalone DC. Original status was ambiguous_candidate. Census onelineaddress returned no match; Nominatim geocoded to the exact building (100 William Street, Financial District) at 40.70834,-74.00717. Satellite shows dense Lower Manhattan grid; point sits on the correct tower. CANDIDATE: correct building well-identified, but colo is interior so no visual DC signature.

Identity confirmed: South Reach Networks (formerly Interconnect Miami) POP at 36 NE 2nd Street, Suite 520, 5th floor, described as Miami's premier carrier hotel and gateway to the Americas/LatAm. In-building colo on the 5th floor of a downtown Miami building. Original status was no_building_found_manual. Census geocode is address-precise (36 NE 2nd St) and lands on the building at image center in the downtown Miami core. CANDIDATE: correct building identified by documented suite address; colo is interior (5th floor) so no rooftop DC signature.

Identity confirmed: IPR Wilmington CCC (now DaSTOR), 1201 N Market Street, Wilmington DE — a 78,000 sqft facility with 23,000 sqft raised floor, Delaware's only Tier III data center, downtown data hub. Census geocode is address-precise and lands on the building at image center in downtown Wilmington. Satellite shows a dense downtown block; the data center occupies a downtown building rather than a standalone campus, so no isolated cooling-yard/substation signature is distinguishable from the rooftop. CANDIDATE: correct building by precise address, sizable dedicated DC, but rooftop not visually confirmatory.

Identity confirmed: Netrality 210 North Tucker Blvd, St. Louis — a 442,837 sqft, 10-story (1923) whole-building carrier hotel / data center, 20 MW, the primary St. Louis interconnection building (acquired from Digital Realty 2016). This is an entire-building data center with a distinct full-block footprint. Census geocode is address-precise; satellite shows the large full-block downtown building at image center matching the documented footprint. Point placed on the building centroid. VERIFIED: well-documented whole-building DC, distinct large footprint, no competing look-alike.

Identity confirmed: TunBroker Baltimore at 813 Pinnacle Drive, Linthicum Heights (BWI Tech Park), same address/building as TierPoint Baltimore-BWI (2.3 MW, chilled-water cooling). Original status was no_building_found_manual. Nominatim geocoded the exact building, labeling it 'TierPoint Baltimore BWI, 813 Pinnacle Drive, BWI Tech Park'. Satellite shows the tech-park building at center-top with clear rooftop cooling/HVAC units (a data-center signal); point moved onto that building's rooftop centroid. Confidence medium because the park contains several similar low-rise buildings, but the rooftop equipment plus building-precise geocode identify the correct one.
